preamble: The spirantization of a palatalized velar stop [kʲ] to a velar or palatal fricative [x]/[ç] is a common lenition process found in Goidelic and Indo-Iranian languages.

examples.note: Slender mutation (lenition) of final palatalized velars.
Historical spirantization in certain phonological contexts within the development of Indo-Iranian.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
